decision_summary: The runtime-generated loop trace shows that the decisive benchmark improvement arrived in loop 3, not merely because multiple loops happened.
rationale: Loop 1 established a baseline positive path and one broken-chain rejection. Loop 2 made the audit evidence more inspectable by adding a machine-readable human-audit packet and score thresholds. Loop 3 is the first loop that materially broadens the negative-path benchmark by adding a second broken-chain family while preserving the positive-path pass. The review therefore distinguishes structural polish from actual coverage gain.
recommendation: Accept this runtime-generated loop trace as review-validity evidence and continue extending the same pattern into review, diagnosis, and outcome fail/reopen traces.
target_audience: A framework operator deciding whether benchmark loops produce substantive review improvements.
expected_user_reaction: The later loop should feel materially more credible because it explains exactly which improvement changed benchmark coverage rather than only adding more artifacts.
artifact_change_recommendations: Preserve machine-readable audit evidence once introduced.
Prefer benchmark improvements that widen actual failure coverage rather than only restating the same pass.
organization_change_recommendations: Require future benchmark improvement claims to identify which loop introduced the substantive correction.
Keep runtime-generated negative-path expansion as a tracked review objective.
human_override_signal: Owner expectation: runtime-generated benchmark improvements should show which loop produced the real correction, not just more artifact volume.
